# llm-cost-optimizer-node (v1.1.3)

> High-performance, offline-first client wrapper proxy and context optimization utility. Minifies prompt strings locally in-memory (<2ms) to compress context windows and drop API costs. Includes non-blocking, background-batched cloud telemetry syncing.

---

## Core API Architecture & Exports

The library exposes three core procedural functional utilities. It does not export classes.

```javascript
const { wrapClient, compressTextLocally, estimateTokens } = require('llm-cost-optimizer-node');
```

## 1. The Proxy Wrapper Pipeline
Signature

```javascript
wrapClient(clientInstance, config)
```

Intercepts outgoing OpenAI chat.completions.create message payload arrays via a JavaScript Proxy mechanism. It compresses message contents synchronously in-memory before forwarding the modified parameters to the destination target.

### Parameters

### Configuration Reference

## `clientInstance` (Object)

An instantiated native OpenAI client or structurally compatible SDK instance exposing:

```javascript
chat.completions.create
```

---

## `config` (Object)

Configuration parameters regulating the local token optimization passes.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mode` | `String` (Optional) | Contextual strategy engine profile. Options: `"chat"` \| `"rag"` \| `"agent"` \| `"codegen"`. Defaults to `"chat"`. |
| `language` | `String` (Optional) | ISO dictionary lookups for stop-word filtering. Options: `"en"` \| `"es"` \| `"fr"`. Defaults to `"en"`. |
| `rapidApiKey` | `String` (Optional) | Gateway key that activates a non-blocking background telemetry reporter loop flushing metrics every `5000ms`. |
| `strategy` | `Array<String>` (Optional) | Manual override array of algorithms to execute. Options: `"minify"` \| `"strip_stopwords"` \| `"stemming"` \| `"strip_boilerplate"`. |
| `ignore_words` | `Array<String>` (Optional) | Custom list of terminology strings explicitly locked down and protected from stop-word or stemming compression rules. |


## Code Sample

```
const { OpenAI } = require('openai');
const { wrapClient } = require('llm-cost-optimizer-node');

const openai = wrapClient(
    new OpenAI({ apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY }),
    {
        mode: "rag",
        language: "en",
        rapidApiKey: process.env.RAPID_API_KEY,
        ignore_words: ["cluster-01", "sharding"]
    }
);

// Downstream execution signatures remain completely unaltered
const response = await openai.chat.completions.create({
    model: "gpt-4o",
    messages: [{ role: "user", content: "Optimize cluster-01 sharding parameters immediately." }]
});
```

## 2. Standalone Utility Functions

For granular data transformations inside vector embeddings, agent lifecycles, or state machine variables.

# Utility Functions

## `compressTextLocally(text, config)`

Executes synchronous linguistic optimization algorithms directly on raw string inputs.

### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `text` | `String` | The raw text block to pass through compression. |
| `config` | `Object` | Matches parameters defined in `wrapClient` (`mode`, `strategy`, `language`, `ignore_words`). |

### Returns

```javascript
String
```

The optimized text.

---

## `estimateTokens(text)`

Executes a high-speed token heuristic calculation using character splits.

### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `text` | `String` | Target text. |

### Returns

```javascript
Number
```

Estimated integer token size.

## Code Sample

```
const { compressTextLocally, estimateTokens } = require('llm-cost-optimizer-node');

const rawContext = "The administrative telemetry configurations are thoroughly optimized.";
const cleanText = compressTextLocally(rawContext, {
    mode: "chat",
    strategy: ["minify", "strip_stopwords"]
});

console.log(`Tokens: ${estimateTokens(rawContext)} -> ${estimateTokens(cleanText)}`);
```

## Workload Engine Profiles

| Mode | Default Strategies | Internal Safeguard Triggers & Regex Behaviors |
|---|---|---|
| `"chat"` | `["minify", "strip_stopwords"]` | Standard conversion filtering. Preserves conversational intent verbs. |
| `"rag"` | `["minify", "strip_stopwords", "stemming"]` | Extracts alphanumeric strings, numbers, values, and identifiers (`/\b\d+[\w-]*\b|\$\d+(?:\.\d+)?/g`) and forces them automatically into `ignore_words` to prevent data loss. |
| `"agent"` | `["minify"]` | Isolates syntactic symbols, formatting control anchors, and JSON boundaries (`/[{}[\]"':,📊🤖⚙️]/g`) and flags them to preserve system block integrity. |
| `"codegen"` | `["minify"]` | Strips out multi-line documentation strings and code comments (`/\*[\s\S]*?\*/`). |

## Telemetry & Runtime Engineering

### Non-Blocking Memory Ring Buffer

When a rapidApiKey configuration parameter is evaluated, a background execution loop starts. Metric tracking payloads are safely enqueued onto an in-memory array queue:

```
[https://llm-token-optimizer-api.vercel.app/api/telemetry](https://llm-token-optimizer-api.vercel.app/api/telemetry)
```

An internal setInterval block batch-flushes this queue every 5000ms. The buffer array is instantly re-instantiated on flush intervals to prevent system memory corruption or race conditions.

Global CLI Binary Interface
* Command: llm-optimize benchmark

* Entry Script Location: ./bin/cli.js

* Behavior: Inks structural layout matrices checking linguistic effectiveness and rule boundaries across modes via terminal standard output channels.

### Production Resiliency Guardrail

The entire wrapper interceptor loop is contained inside an explicit global try/catch block. If an underlying network exception, empty dependency variant, or structural type mismatch occurs, the engine triggers a non-blocking console.warn message and engages a silent pass-through bypass layer. The original string data structures are delivered to the target LLM completely unchanged, ensuring 0% application runtime execution downtime.
